summary: base58-core: clipboard wallet-address hijacker with remote exfiltration and shell persistence
details: Package advertises itself as a Base58 encoder/decoder but on require schedules a clipboard monitor that, after a 72-hour activation delay, polls the system clipboard every 2.5 seconds for Bitcoin/Ethereum/Solana addresses, WIF/hex private keys, and BIP-39 seed phrases. Matches, together with hostname, platform, and current working directory, are POSTed to a hardcoded remote endpoint at http://2.27.62.51:8080/api/health (with a secondary port:8081). When a cryptocurrency address is detected in the clipboard, the package overwrites it with hardcoded attacker-controlled wallet addresses (BTC: bc1qjft978uykglsh0adcyx6xhkes56vqzs3fual3l, ETH: 0xd63eD44065eDb1e2ad2519B011c06412dA7B7c5B, SOL: A7ajd7W5WYdrnkeaiBRjVoK6uBEDvgnuZcpzQXqo18Ph), causing the victim to paste the attacker's address into wallet/exchange transfers. The package establishes persistence by appending a re-invocation hook to ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, and ~/.profile, and on Windows drops a.js launcher into the Startup folder that re-invokes the package's _internal.activate at every login. dist/index.cjs and dist/index.js use child_process.execSync('powershell...') for Windows clipboard access. The 72-hour activation delay is an explicit anti-analysis measure to evade sandbox scanners. The package name impersonates the legitimate base58 ecosystem to lure web3 / wallet developers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
